content.text:
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:LvPkfj | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Features
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:LvPkfj | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Quick Start
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:LvPkfj | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:LvPkfj | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | The script will:
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:LvPkfj | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Run the application
| MCP:LvPkfj | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | docker compose up -d
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Project Structure
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:LvPkfj | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:LvPkfj | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:LvPkfj | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── config/
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:LvPkfj | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:LvPkfj | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:LvPkfj | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## API Usage
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:LvPkfj | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:LvPkfj | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:LvPkfj | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:LvPkfj | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:LvPkfj | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```java
| MCP:LvPkfj | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:LvPkfj | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:LvPkfj | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:LvPkfj | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Idempotency
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```java
| MCP:LvPkfj | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:LvPkfj | @Idempotent
| MCP:LvPkfj | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:LvPkfj | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:LvPkfj | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```sh
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Run test environment
| MCP:LvPkfj | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Run prod environment
| MCP:LvPkfj | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:LvPkfj | ```
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Contributing
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup instructions, code style, and PR process.
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ---
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | ## License
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.
| MCP:LvPkfj | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: dc8f4b00ea766a399794f53c47719882381c1133)

| MCP:LvPkfj | package com.tzesh.springtemplate.enumeration.auth;
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | import lombok.Getter;
| MCP:LvPkfj | import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | /**
| MCP:LvPkfj |  * Permission is an enum for permissions
| MCP:LvPkfj |  * @author tzesh
| MCP:LvPkfj |  */
| MCP:LvPkfj | @RequiredArgsConstructor
| MCP:LvPkfj | public enum Permission {
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj |     ADMIN_READ("admin:read"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     ADMIN_UPDATE("admin:update"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     ADMIN_CREATE("admin:create"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     ADMIN_DELETE("admin:delete"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     MANAGER_READ("management:read"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     MANAGER_UPDATE("management:update"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     MANAGER_CREATE("management:create"),
| MCP:LvPkfj |     MANAGER_DELETE("management:delete");
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj |     @Getter
| MCP:LvPkfj |     private final String permission;
| MCP:LvPkfj | }

| MCP:LvPkfj | ## Base Configuration File for Spring Boot Template API
| MCP:LvPkfj | # ==========================
| MCP:LvPkfj | # This file contains the base configuration for the Spring Boot Template API.
| MCP:LvPkfj | # It includes settings for server, application, database, JPA, and JWT.
| MCP:LvPkfj | # ==========================
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Note: This file is intended to be used as a template. Modify the values as needed for your environment.
| MCP:LvPkfj | # ==========================
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Spring Boot Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | server.port=8080
| MCP:LvPkfj | server.servlet.context-path=/api/v1/
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Application Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | application.title=Spring Template
| MCP:LvPkfj | application.version=2.0
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # JPA Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # JWT Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | security.jwt.secret-key=34743777217A25432A462D4A614E645267556B58703273357638782F413F4428472B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A244226452948404D635166546A57
| MCP:LvPkfj | security.jwt.authorization-key=614E645267556B58703273357638792F423F4528482B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A24432646294A404E635166546A576E5A7234753778214125442A
| MCP:LvPkfj | security.jwt.expiration=86400000
| MCP:LvPkfj | security.jwt.refresh-expiration=604800000
| MCP:LvPkfj | security.jwt.issuer=SpringTemplate
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Rate Limit Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.enabled=true
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.limit=100
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Rate Limit Category Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.strict.limit=10
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.strict.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.standard.limit=60
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.standard.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.relaxed.limit=200
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.relaxed.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.authentication.limit=5
| MCP:LvPkfj | ratelimit.authentication.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Redis Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.data.redis.host=redis
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.data.redis.port=6379
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Spring Session with Redis
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.session.store-type=redis
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.session.redis.namespace=spring:session
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.session.timeout=30m
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Kafka Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers=kafka:9092
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.consumer.group-id=spring-template-group
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset=earliest
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.consumer.key-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.consumer.value-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.producer.key-serializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
| MCP:LvPkfj | spring.kafka.producer.value-serializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringSerializer
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Kafka Logging Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | logging.level.org.apache.kafka=INFO
| MCP:LvPkfj | logging.level.org.springframework.kafka=INFO
| MCP:LvPkfj | # To enable debug logging for troubleshooting, use DEBUG instead of INFO
| MCP:LvPkfj | # logging.level.org.apache.kafka=DEBUG
| MCP:LvPkfj | # logging.level.org.springframework.kafka=DEBUG
| MCP:LvPkfj | 
| MCP:LvPkfj | # Actuator Configuration
| MCP:LvPkfj | management.endpoints.web.exposure.include=health,info,metrics
| MCP:LvPkfj | management.endpoint.health.show-details=when-authorized
| MCP:LvPkfj | management.endpoint.health.probes.enabled=true

data.content:
Based on my analysis of the repository, here's a comprehensive explanation of how this Spring Boot template handles security and protected API usage:

## Security Architecture Overview

This template implements a **stateless JWT-based authentication system** with role-based access control, built on Spring Security 6.0+ with method-level security annotations.

### 1. JWT Authentication Flow

**Token Generation & Validation:**
- **Secret Key**: Configured via `security.jwt.secret-key` in `application.properties` (Base64-encoded HMAC-SHA key)
- **Token Types**: Access tokens (24-hour expiration) and refresh tokens (7-day expiration)
- **Issuer**: Configurable (`SpringTemplate` by default)

**Authentication Process:**
1. **Registration** (`POST /auth/register`): Creates a new user with `USER` role, returns JWT + refresh token
2. **Login** (`POST /auth/login`): Authenticates credentials via `AuthenticationManager`, revokes previous tokens, issues new JWT + refresh token
3. **Token Refresh** (`POST /auth/refresh-token`): Validates refresh token, issues new access token

**Token Storage & Validation:**
- Tokens are stored in the database via `TokenRepository` with `expired` and `revoked` flags
- On each request, `AuthenticationFilter` extracts the JWT from `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
- Validates token signature, expiration, and checks if token is not revoked/expired in database

### 2. Role-Based Access Control

**Roles & Permissions:**
```java
USER  → No management permissions (default registration role)
MANAGER → MANAGER_READ, MANAGER_UPDATE, MANAGER_DELETE, MANAGER_CREATE
ADMIN → All permissions (both ADMIN_* and MANAGER_*)
```

**URL-Based Security Rules (from `SecurityChain.java`):**
- **Public endpoints**: `/auth/**`, Swagger UI, Actuator endpoints
- **Admin/Manager endpoints**: `/management/**` requires `ADMIN` or `MANAGER` role
- **Method-level restrictions**: Different HTTP methods require specific permissions:
  - GET → `ADMIN_READ` or `MANAGER_READ`
  - POST → `ADMIN_CREATE` or `MANAGER_CREATE`
  - PUT/PATCH → `ADMIN_UPDATE` or `MANAGER_UPDATE`
  - DELETE → `ADMIN_DELETE` or `MANAGER_DELETE`
- **All other endpoints**: Require authentication (any authenticated user)

### 3. Security Configuration Details

**Stateless Sessions:**
```java
.sessionManagement(session -> session.sessionCreationPolicy(SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS))
```
- No server-side sessions; each request must include a valid JWT

**Logout Handling:**
- `POST /auth/logout` revokes the current JWT in the database
- Clears the security context

**Authentication Entry Point:**
- Custom `AuthenticationEntryPoint` returns 401 Unauthorized with proper error response for unauthenticated requests

### 4. User Interaction with Secured Endpoints

**Step-by-Step Usage:**

1. **Register or Login:**
   ```bash
   # Register
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/register \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"username":"user1","email":"user1@example.com","password":"password","name":"User One"}'
   
   # Login
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/login \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"username":"user1","password":"password"}'
   ```

2. **Use the JWT Token:**
   ```bash
   # Access protected endpoint
   curl -X GET http://localhost:8080/api/v1/users \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>"
   ```

3. **Role Promotion (Admin Only):**
   ```bash
   # Promote user to ADMIN role (requires secret key)
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/authorize \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"username":"user1","role":"ADMIN","secret":"<authorization-key>"}'
   ```

4. **Token Refresh:**
   ```bash
   curl -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/refresh-token \
     -H "Authorization: Bearer <refresh-token>"
   ```

### 5. Additional Security Features

**Rate Limiting:**
- Authentication endpoints: 5 requests/minute
- Standard endpoints: 60 requests/minute
- Strict endpoints: 10 requests/minute

**Idempotency:**
- Registration endpoint supports idempotency keys to prevent duplicate registrations

**Password Security:**
- Passwords are hashed using BCrypt before storage

**Token Revocation:**
- On login, all previous tokens for the user are revoked
- Individual token revocation on logout

### 6. Swagger UI Integration

- Access Swagger UI at: `http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html`
- Use the "Authorize" button to input your JWT token
- All secured endpoints will show lock icons
- Test protected endpoints directly from the UI

### 7. Environment Configuration

The security configuration supports multiple environments:
- **Development** (`.env.dev`): Default configuration
- **Test** (`.env.test`): For testing
- **QA** (`.env.qa`): Quality assurance
- **Production** (`.env.prod`): Production settings

Each environment can have different JWT secret keys and expiration times.

This template provides a production-ready security foundation that follows modern best practices: stateless authentication, granular role-based access control, token revocation, and comprehensive protection against common security vulnerabilities.
